title: crepidomanes plicatum
intro: Crepidomanes plicatum, also known as the folded netvein fern, is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It is a small, delicate plant with folded, net-veined fronds that are often found growing on trees in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, Crepidomanes plicatum has been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are believed to provide health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
